new_papers.title: Locating and Editing Factual Associations in GPT (ROME)
new_papers.summary: Causal tracing localizes factual recall to mid-layer MLPs at the last subject token in GPT-2 XL. Introduces the three-run (clean / corrupted / corrupted-with-restoration) activation-patching protocol that became standard. Validates localization with Rank-One Model Editing (ROME): closed-form rank-one MLP weight update achieves 99.8% efficacy / 88% paraphrase on zsRE editing. Note: this entry supersedes the existing `meng2022-rome` placeholder in papers.json — same key, now has local_file + excerpts_file.

new_papers.title: Interpretability in the Wild: a Circuit for Indirect Object Identification in GPT-2 Small
new_papers.summary: Reverse-engineers the IOI task in GPT-2 small to a 26-head circuit in 7 functional classes (Duplicate Token, Previous Token, S-Inhibition, Name Mover, Negative Name Mover, Backup Name Mover, Induction). Introduces *path patching* and the faithfulness/completeness/minimality criteria for circuit validity. Note: this entry supersedes the existing `wang2022-ioi` placeholder in papers.json — same key, now has local_file + excerpts_file + topics.

new_papers.title: Towards Best Practices of Activation Patching in Language Models: Metrics and Methods
new_papers.summary: First systematic methodology study of activation patching: shows the choice of corruption method (Gaussian Noising vs. Symmetric Token Replacement) and metric (probability vs. logit difference vs. KL) materially changes which components get implicated. Recommends STR + logit difference as default. The paper that turned activation patching from artisanal to documented protocol.

new_papers.title: How to Use and Interpret Activation Patching
new_papers.summary: Practitioner's guide to activation patching written by Anthropic and independent researchers. Pedagogical companion to Zhang & Nanda 2023. Not yet excerpted — Phase 2 follow-up.

new_papers.title: Towards Automated Circuit Discovery for Mechanistic Interpretability (ACDC)
new_papers.summary: Automated Circuit DisCovery: iteratively path-patches every edge in the model's computational graph, prunes edges with patching effect below threshold, returns the resulting subgraph. First fully-automated mech-interp circuit discovery method. Not yet excerpted — Phase 2 follow-up.

new_papers.title: Jumping Ahead: Improving Reconstruction Fidelity with JumpReLU Sparse Autoencoders
new_papers.authors: Rajamanoharan, Lieberum, Sonnerat, Conmy, Varma, Kramár, Nanda
new_papers.summary: JumpReLU SAE: replace SAE encoder ReLU with a discontinuous JumpReLU activation, train with L0 penalty (not L1), use straight-through estimators (STEs) to backprop through the discontinuity. SOTA reconstruction at fixed L0 on Gemma 2 9B; matches/beats TopK with cheaper training (no partial sort). The SAE architecture used in Gemma Scope.

new_papers.title: Scaling and evaluating sparse autoencoders
new_papers.authors: Gao, Dupré la Tour, Tillman, Goh, Troll, Radford, Sutskever, Leike, Wu
new_papers.summary: OpenAI TopK SAEs + scaling laws L(C) and L(N, K) for SAE training. Trains a 16M-latent SAE on GPT-4 activations for 40B tokens (~20% of GPT-3 pretraining compute). Establishes auxiliary AuxK loss for dead-latent prevention; ~7% dead latents in 16M-latent SAE.

new_papers.title: Gemma Scope: Open Sparse Autoencoders Everywhere All At Once on Gemma 2
new_papers.authors: Lieberum, Rajamanoharan, Conmy, Smith, Sonnerat, Varma, Kramár, Dragan, Shah, Nanda
new_papers.summary: Open release of >2,000 JumpReLU SAEs covering all layers and sublayers (residual, MLP-out, attn-out) of Gemma 2 2B and 9B + select layers of 27B. >30M total learned features; trained on 4-16B tokens each. Includes one suite of transcoders. Note: this entry partially overlaps with the existing `gemma-scope-2024` key in papers.json — same key, now has local_file. Not yet excerpted in this pass — quoted via the JumpReLU companion paper.

new_papers.title: Transcoders Find Interpretable LLM Feature Circuits
new_papers.summary: Transcoders: a wide ReLU MLP that learns to mimic the input/output behavior of an MLP sublayer with sparse hidden activations. Unlike SAEs (which decode activation back to itself), transcoders give an *input-invariant* factorization of MLP computations into sparse feature circuits. Architectural primitive of Anthropic's 2025 attribution-graph framework.

new_papers.title: Sparse Autoencoders Do Not Find Canonical Units of Analysis
new_papers.authors: Leask, Bussmann, Pearce, Bloom, Tigges, Al Moubayed, Sharkey, Nanda
new_papers.summary: Negative result: SAE stitching shows larger SAEs find features absent from smaller ones (incompleteness); meta-SAEs show larger latents decompose into combinations of smaller latents (non-atomicity). Direct contradiction with the 'true features' framing of Bricken 2023 / Templeton 2024. Note: this entry supersedes the existing `sae-not-canonical-2025` placeholder in papers.json — same key, now has local_file + excerpts_file + correct authors. The arxiv ID for this paper is 2502.04878 (the URL in the existing papers.json entry was an ICLR PDF link, which is also valid).

new_papers.title: Eliciting Latent Predictions from Transformers with the Tuned Lens
new_papers.authors: Belrose, Furman, McKinney, Halawi, Smith, Biderman, Steinhardt, Ostrovsky
new_papers.summary: Trained affine 'translators' per layer that map intermediate activations into the basis the unembedding expects. More reliable than the raw logit lens, especially on BLOOM/GPT-Neo. Introduces Causal Basis Extraction (CBE) for finding influential residual-stream directions. Note: this entry supersedes the existing `belrose2023-tuned-lens` placeholder in papers.json — same key, now has local_file.

new_papers.title: The Geometry of Truth: Emergent Linear Structure in Large Language Model Representations of True/False Datasets
new_papers.summary: Mass-mean probing for truth: the 'truth direction' is the difference between mean activations on true vs. false statements. Generalizes across logical transformations where logistic-regression probes fail. Note: Phase 1 sweep mis-attributed this to 'Burns et al.' — actual authors are Samuel Marks and Max Tegmark. The unrelated `burns2023-w2s` paper is the Burns et al. (OpenAI Superalignment) weak-to-strong generalization paper, in the alignment area.

new_papers.title: In-context Learning and Induction Heads
new_papers.authors: Olsson, Elhage, Nanda, Joseph, DasSarma, Henighan, Mann, Askell, Bai, Chen, Conerly, Drain, Ganguli, Hatfield-Dodds, Hernandez, Johnston, Jones, Kernion, Lovitt, Ndousse, Amodei, Brown, Clark, Kaplan, McCandlish, Olah
new_papers.summary: Induction heads as core ICL mechanism. Six lines of evidence (phase change in loss aligned with phase change in induction-head formation; ablation removes ICL; etc.). HTML-only at transformer-circuits.pub — Phase 2 follow-up: manual transcription or off-sandbox fetch needed for verbatim excerpts.

new_papers.title: Circuit Tracing: Revealing Computational Graphs in Language Models
new_papers.summary: Cross-layer transcoders + Jacobian-based attribution graphs. End-to-end automated circuit discovery on production Claude 3.5 Haiku. Synthesis of SAEs (features) + activation patching (causal intervention). HTML-only at transformer-circuits.pub — Phase 2 follow-up needed for verbatim excerpts. Note: this supersedes the existing `lindsey2025-circuit-tracing` placeholder in papers.json — same key, marked HTML-only.

new_papers.title: On the Biology of a Large Language Model
new_papers.summary: Application of circuit tracing to Claude 3.5 Haiku. Documents forward-planning in poetry (rhyme target features activate before the rhyme word), multi-hop reasoning chains, refusal circuits, language-routing in non-English contexts. HTML-only — Phase 2 follow-up needed.

phase1_corrections.issue: Phase 1 landscape sweep cited 'Burns et al. (NYU/Columbia)' as authors of 'The Geometry of Truth'. The actual authors are Samuel Marks (NYU) and Max Tegmark (MIT) — there is no overlap with the OpenAI Superalignment 'Burns et al.' weak-to-strong paper (`burns2023-w2s`).
phase1_corrections.resolution: New paper key proposed: `marks-tegmark-2023-truth`. The Phase 1 entry `burns2023-truth-geometry` should not be promoted to papers.json; use the new key instead.

phase1_corrections.issue: Phase 1 landscape report flagged the SAE-canonicality contradiction; Phase 2 confirms it but adds nuance — the field has largely moved to a 'pragmatic' position (use SAEs as a useful, basis-dependent tool) rather than abandoning them. Lindsey 2025 explicitly takes this stance.
